hero.bio: Temo Rekhviashvili is a Georgian writer, playwright and actor working across literature, theatre and cinema. He is the author of Courier’s Tales, winner of the 2022 SABA Literary Award for Best Debut, and co-writer and lead actor of Temo Re, winner of the Tiger Short Award and the KNF Award at the International Film Festival Rotterdam. And Very Good Guy

books.label: Novel / Short Novel
books.meta_line2: Winner of the SABA Literary Award for Best Debut, 2022
paragraphs: Courier’s Tales is the debut prose work by Temo Rekhviashvili. Told through the voice of a young actor who works as a courier, the book is composed of fragments from everyday life: deliveries, theatre rehearsals, friendships, debt, exhaustion, self-irony and the effort to survive.
Set mostly in contemporary Tbilisi, the novel moves through streets, staircases, rehearsal rooms and precarious jobs. In its final part, the story opens toward Germany.
books.title: Courier’s Tales

books.label: Plays / Collection
books.meta_line2: A collection of two plays by Temo Rekhviashvili
paragraphs: Pathetic Monologues — Two Plays brings together two stage works by Temo Rekhviashvili, rooted in the language, contradictions and absurdities of contemporary Georgian life.

Pathetic Monologues is a satire of public speech, privilege, politics and self-importance. CO₂ is a darkly comic play about masculinity, alcoholism, failed intimacy and the emotional wreckage left inside domestic life.
books.title: Pathetic Monologues — Two Plays

books.label: Poetry
paragraphs: A collection of poems built from toasts, love, debt, insomnia, pop songs and the small rituals of everyday life.
books.title: Neo-Tbilisian Mukhambazebi

theatre_plays.description: A stage satire of public speech, privilege, politics and self-importance, but also a play about exhaustion, failed intimacy and the fantasy of escape. The production brings together a chorus of voices caught between confession, performance and social absurdity.
theatre_plays.title: Pathetic Monologues

theatre_plays.description: A darkly comic and self-reflective play about masculinity, alcoholism, failed intimacy and the emotional wreckage left inside domestic life. Through a distinctly feminist lens, the play looks at male vulnerability, dependency and the damage men cause while trying — often too late — to understand themselves.

details.value: Friedrich Ebert Stiftung Prize in Dramaturgy, 2025

theatre_plays.title: CO₂

theatre_credits.title: The Tenant
theatre_credits.detail: 2022 · The Tenant / Varden Mekvabishvili / Kote Enukidze

cinema_featured.label: Featured Film
cinema_featured.title: Temo Re
paragraphs: Temo Re is a Georgian short film directed by Anka Gujabidze and adapted from Temo Rekhviashvili’s debut novel Courier’s Tales. Rekhviashvili co-wrote the film and plays the leading role.

The film follows Temo, a young actor who also works as a courier. Over the course of a single day, he travels across Tbilisi on a moped and performs in a suburban theatre in the evening. Poverty, injustice, corruption and alienation gradually turn into disturbing visions.
Constructed as a black-and-white photomontage, Temo Re combines fiction and documentary elements to create a fragmented portrait of contemporary Tbilisi.

details.value: Temo Rekhviashvili, Anka Gujabidze, Salome Asatiani

about.intro: Temo Rekhviashvili is a Georgian writer, playwright and actor working across literature, theatre and cinema.
paragraphs: He was born in 1993 and graduated from the Drama Faculty of Shota Rustaveli Theatre and Film Georgia State University in 2015.

He has worked as an independent freelance actor across several theatres. In 2016, he took part in the founding of Open Space for Experimental Art, and in 2019 became one of the co-founders of Theatre Company Haraki.
In 2017, together with Gvantsa Enukidze, he founded the secret poetry group Universally Accessible Dramatic Poetry. Since 2019, he has been a member of Temur Chkheidze’s Studio.
His debut short novel Courier’s Tales was published by Sulakauri Publishing in 2021 and received the SABA Literary Award for Best Debut in 2022.

He is also the author of Pathetic Monologues — Two Plays, published by Sulakauri Publishing in 2025. Courier’s Tales later inspired the short film Temo Re, directed by Anka Gujabidze, which Rekhviashvili co-wrote and in which he played the leading role.
about.closing: His work is marked by directness, dark humour and close attention to people trying to survive without losing the ability to laugh, desire, remember and speak.
